message:
fix(export-html): drop entire skill messages instead of redacting content

BREAKING CHANGE in approach: the previous strip_skill_and_system_content()
function tried to pattern-match and redact specific phrases within skill
messages. This was fundamentally broken — it left structural content,
headers, code blocks, table rows, and other proprietary material visible
even after "redaction". The function has been deleted entirely.

New approach: when a message contains ANY of these markers, the ENTIRE
message is dropped (return None in the filter_map). No parsing, no
redaction, no placeholders. The message simply does not exist in the
export output.

Drop triggers (any one is sufficient):
  - "Base directory for this skill:" (skill body injection)
  - "<system-reminder>" (hook metadata, skill listings)
  - "The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:"
  - "skillInjection:" + "matchedSkills" (Vercel plugin metadata)
  - "<!-- skillInjection:" (HTML comment injection)

All gated behind !include_skills (default: strip). Use --include-skills
to opt in to preserving skill content in exports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
